classes.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._log
documentation:
Write to offload log file.
no_log_items: See self._validate_no_log_items() for description
PRIVATE METHODS

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._log_or_not
documentation: Wrapper over _log() to cater for suppressing logging of SQLs

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._apply_decimal_padding_digits
documentation:
Add decimal_padding_digits to scale if precision has headroom then pad precision if it has headroom for
that too.
Currently this has no max for scale, i.e. assumes the max is same as max precision. This may not be true
for all backends.
Returns a tuple of the adjusted precision & scale

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._create_table_columns_clause_common
documentation: Trivial helper for SQL text consistency

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._create_table_column_nn_clause_common
documentation:
Return clause for a NOT NULL column if the backend supports it and it is not an external table.
We don't want the constraint on external tables because we want to stage the data and then validate correctness.
nullable of None is treated as True. i.e. we can't be sure of NOT NULL so go with the safe option.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._cursor_projection
documentation:
Returns a list of strings describing the projection of a cursor.
Names are coerced to lower case to give a standard output across different backend systems.
This should be called after the query has been executed.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._cursor_row_to_dict
documentation:
Trivial function combining typical tuple/list row format with results of _cursor_projection() to
generate a row dict.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._fixed_session_parameters
documentation: Dictionary of session parameters for a given backend. This top level method gives us an empty base

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._format_select_projection
documentation: Return string of column-expr AS alias pairs formatted consistently across all usages

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._gen_max_column_values_sql
documentation:
Produce a SQL statement that selects a MAX row from the underlying table.
Returns None if no data selected.
The columns_to_cast_to_string parameter is because our HiveServer2 DBAPI does not support nanoseconds
therefore we bring certain data types back as strings to avoid truncating to milliseconds.
filter_clause: Option WHERE clause

NOTE: Some backends (e.g. Synapse) may override this if the "common" syntax used here is incompatible.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._gen_global_session_parameters
documentation:
Merge user defined session parameters with our fixed session parameters.
We remove any fixed parameters from custom list in a case insensitive way to prevent duplicates.
Apply fixed parameters second so they have precedence.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._gen_sql_text_common
documentation:
Generate a SQL statement appropriate for running in the backend.
This is common functionality for multiple backends, individual backends may override it.
See gen_sql_text() description for parameter descriptions.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._gen_select_count_sql_text_common
documentation:
filter_clause does NOT include "WHERE" because that could be backend specific.
At the point WHERE is not compatible with a backend we'll need to add an
override method for this.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._get_max_column_values_common
documentation:
Get max value for a set of columns.
optimistic_prune_clause is used to limit the partitions scanned when looking for existing rows.
If no data is returned then we fall back to scanning without the clause.
optimistic_prune_clause is only applicable for single column partition schemes.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._insert_literals_using_insert_values_sql_text
documentation: Format a SQL statement for inserting literals using SQL where multiple rows are passed to VALUES.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._run_os_cmd
documentation:
Run an os command and:
  - Throw an exception if the return code is non-zero.
  - Put a dot on stdout for each line logged to give the impression of progress.
  - Logs all command output at VVERBOSE level.
silent: No stdout unless vverbose is requested.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._table_partition_info
documentation:
We need a standard value to return from get_table_partitions() so we know what data to expect.
All inputs, except partition_id, are optional because different backends will have different details.
This method is to be used on a per partition basis with the intention that combined we'll have a dict
of all partitions with the partition_id as the key as well as an attribute.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._validate_no_log_items
documentation:
Validate no_log_items is a valid dict for the purpose of removing sensitive information from logging.
no_log_items should be either:
    {'item': 'something-sensitive-to-replace', 'sub': 'replace-with-what'}
or
    [
        {'item': 'something-sensitive-to-replace', 'sub': 'replace-with-what'},
        ... more dicts ...
    ]
'sub' is optional

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._backend_capabilities
documentation: enforced methods

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._execute_ddl_or_dml
documentation:
Run SQL that has no result set, e.g. DDL, sql can be a str or a list of SQLs.
This function returns the SQL statements that were executed.
Exception will be raised upon failure.
Parameters:
    sync: None means do nothing. True/False mean act as appropriate.
    query_options: key/value pairs for session settings.
    profile: if True we will log a profile (if possible) *after the first* SQL statement.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._execute_global_session_parameters
documentation: Use this to ensure global session parameters requested by the user are honoured in each backend.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._gen_sample_stats_sql_sample_clause
documentation:
Return a sample clause to slot in after a FROM table clause.
Some backends may have a completely different structure so this may need refactoring in the future.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface._get_query_profile
documentation:
Return a string containing query details of last query executed.
Optional query_id parameter for backends that need more information.
This is defined as private because backends are inconsistent so we can't have a standard approach.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.backend_db_name
documentation: Some backends have overrides due to stylistic formatting, e.g. BigQuery.
PUBLIC METHODS

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.decimal_scale_validation_expression
documentation:
Return a SQL expression to validate the scale length of string based staging columns holding decimal data.
The expression ignores data in scientific notation because the scale in those literals bears no relation
to the actual scale of the value.
Some backends have overrides due to lack of regular expression support, e.g. Synapse

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.execute_ddl
documentation:
Simple wrapper over _execute_ddl_or_dml() to ensure we always get a return value.
See self._validate_no_log_items() for expected use/format.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.extract_date_part_sql_expression
documentation:
Return a SQL expression that can be used to extract DAY, MONTH or YEAR from a column value.
Most backends have an EXTRACT(x FROM c) function which is what this method returns. Some backends
may override with their own variation.
column: Can be a column object or a column name.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.format_column_comparison
documentation:
Format a simple 'column operator column' string, generic for most backends but some may override.
left_col and right_col are column objects, not names.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.get_column
documentation: Get a single column from get_columns()

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.get_distinct_expressions
documentation:
Run SQL to get distinct expressions from a table.
Currently common across all backends but could deviate in the future.
All current backend support this syntax:
    SELECT DISTINCT expr AS alias FROM db.table ORDER BY alias

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.get_max_column_length
documentation:
Select the length of every value in table_name.column_name and return the max value.
Useful when trying to infer a suitable column size when presenting to an RDBMS.
Currently the simple SQL is valid in all backends, this may change in time and need
to become an abstractmethod.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.identifier_contains_invalid_characters
documentation:
Checks that characters in identifier are valid for the backend system
Returns a list of invalid characters which allows truthy use and use of details in logging

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.is_synthetic_partition_column
documentation:
Is a column a synthetic partition column - based on its name.
column can be a column object or a name.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.max_column_name_length
documentation:
The maximum number of characters permitted in a column name.
For most engines this is the same but some may require an implementation level override.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.max_db_name_length
documentation:
The maximum number of characters permitted in a database/dataset/schema name.
For most engines this is the same but some may require an implementation level override.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.partition_range_max
documentation:
Maximum supported value for a numeric partition key range.
Defaults to None (meaning not applicable) in this interface but some implementations may override.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.partition_range_min
documentation:
Minimum supported value for a numeric partition key range.
Defaults to None (meaning not applicable) in this interface but some implementations may override.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.regexp_extract_decimal_scale_pattern
documentation:
Pattern to extract the scale from a numeric value inside a string.
Exists as it's own function purely so we can unit test it.
Searches for:
    ^        Start of line anchor
    -?       Optional sign
    [0-9]*   Any numeric digits (or perhaps no digits in case of numbers like -.0123)
    [.]      Mandatory decimal place, no backslashes because backends are inconsistent inside strings
    ([0-9]+) Mandatory group matching the decimal places
    $        End of line anchor

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.add_columns
documentation:
column_tuples: A list of tuples of the format:
[
    (col_name1, data_type),
    (col_name2, data_type),
]
enforced methods/properties

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.backend_report_info
documentation:
Reporting information about the type of backend and how to display
its configuration details (e.g. Hadoop host:port or BigQuery project).

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.backend_version
documentation:
Output some descriptive information about the backend
This is different to target_version() even though it appears similar in function.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.check_backend_supporting_objects
goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.close
documentation: Free up any resources currently held

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.compute_stats
documentation:
Currently doesn't cater for partition specific commands, this will need to be added in the near future
for_columns: compute stats at column level rather than table level, ignored on some backends
partition_tuples: A list of tuples of the format:
    [
        (part_col_name1, col_value),
        (part_col_name2, col_value),
    ]

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.create_database
documentation:
Create a backend database or equivalent container for tables (such as dataset or schema).
properties: An optional dictionary to pass information to different backends, e.g.:
            properties={"location": "us-west"}
            Enables a BackendTable implementation to pass implementation specifics to BackendApi

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.create_table
documentation:
Create a table and cater for backend specific details, we need to pass all parameters even if they
don't apply to certain backends but some may be ignored depending on the system involved:
    column_list: A list of column objects for the table.
    partition_column_names: A list of column names for table partitioning, the columns must also
                            be in column_list.
    location: The location URI for Hadoop based systems
    table_properties: In Hadoop these are TBLPROPERTIES, in BigQuery table options
    sort_column_names: For systems that can influence data order by defining it when a table is created
    without_db_name: In very special cases we allow a CREATE TABLE to run without the db name
Passing the various parameters as something false-like will exclude the clause from the SQL

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.create_view
documentation:
Create a backend view
column_tuples: A list of (column expression, alias) tuples
filter_clauses: A list of 'col=something' strings
ansi_joined_tables: contents of the from clause which could be
                        db.table
                    or
                        db.t1 inner join db.t2 on (t1.id = t2.id)
                    (there's an assumption here that backend systems understand ANSI
                     SQL - this may need to change)
Functions should not enclose components of column_tuples/ansi_joined_tables/filter_clauses identifiers
because they might be expressions with identifiers already enclosed.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.current_date_sql_expression
documentation: Return a SQL expression that can be used to get the current date, probably a SQL function name

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.data_type_accepts_length
documentation: Returns true when a data type accepts a length in its SQL spec, e.g. VARCHAR2(10) or NUMBER(5)

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.db_name_label
documentation: Return the correct name for whatever we use db_name for in the backend, e.g. database/schema/etc

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.default_date_based_partition_granularity
documentation:
Return default granularity for date based partitioning.
Should be one of:
  PART_COL_GRANULARITY_YEAR, PART_COL_GRANULARITY_MONTH, PART_COL_GRANULARITY_DAY

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.default_storage_format
documentation:
This is a staticmethod in subclasses but Python 2.7 does not have abstractstaticmethod.
This is correct to be static because it is used before we have a connection.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.derive_native_partition_info
documentation: Return a ColumnPartitionInfo object (or None) based on native partition settings

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.detect_column_has_fractional_seconds
documentation:
Detect whether a backend date based column contains fractional seconds.
Returns True/False.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.drop_state
documentation: Drop any cached state so values are refreshed on next request.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.drop_table
documentation:
Drop a table and cater for backend specific details:
purge: some backends have external tables where we also need to clean up the filesystem.
sync: some backends are able to run DDL sync or async.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.enclose_identifier
documentation: Surround an identifier in the correct character for the backend system.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.enclose_object_reference
documentation:
Correctly use enclose_identifier() on a composite reference to an object.
For example:
    Hadoop: `db`.`object`
    BigQuery: `ds.object`

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.enclosure_character
documentation:
The character to use in enclose_identifier/enclose_object_reference.
Superceded by enclose_identifier(), ideally we shouldn't use enclosure_character() any longer.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.execute_query_fetch_all
documentation: Run a query and fetch all rows

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.execute_query_fetch_one
documentation: Run a query and fetch one row

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.execute_query_get_cursor
documentation:
Run a query and return the cursor to allow the calling program to handle fetching.
execute_query_get_cursor() exposes too lower a level of detail to higher level code.
Strongly advise NOT to implement/use this method.
For parameter descriptions see: _execute_query_fetch_x.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.exists
documentation: Does the table/view exist

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.format_query_parameter
documentation:
For backends that support query parameters this method prefixes or suffixes a parameter name.
For example on BigQuery it prefixes an @ symbol.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.gen_column_object
documentation: Returns a column object of the correct type for the backend system

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.gen_copy_into_sql_text
documentation:
Build an INSERT/SELECT SQL statement and return the resulting string.
Most backends will only use gen_insert_select_sql_text() but some have a COPY INTO SQL command.
select_expr_tuples: A list of tuples containing: (the-SQL-expression-to-select, the-column-name).
from_object_clause: The name/expression to select from, could be db.table_name or something more complex.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.gen_ctas_sql_text
documentation:
Generate and return SQL text used to create a table via CTAS
column_tuples: A list of (column expression, alias) tuples
from_db_name/table_name: db and table to select from, otherwise select a single row in the standard
                         way for the backend
row_limit: Apply a row limit to the select part of the statement
external: create an external table or not
table_properties: table properties as a dictionary

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.gen_default_numeric_column
documentation:
Return a default best-fit numeric column for generic usage
Used when we can't really know or influence a type, e.g. AVG() output in AAPD
data_scale should have an appropriate default for each backend (None used above for illustration)
data_scale=0 can be used if the outcome is known to be integral

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.gen_insert_select_sql_text
documentation:
Build an INSERT/SELECT SQL statement and return the resulting string.
select_expr_tuples: A list of tuples containing: (the-SQL-expression-to-select, the-column-name).
partition_expr_tuples: (the-SQL-expression-to-select, the-partition-column-name).
Note: partition_expr_tuples is only needed when the backend must process them independently, for example
      in Hadoop they are virtual and do not really exist. Most other systems will not need this parameter.
sort_expr_list: A list of expressions by which to sort incoming data.
distribute_columns: A list of column names to include in any distribute clause.
from_object_override: An override for from_db/table_name, in some cases we need a more complex row source.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.gen_native_range_partition_key_cast
documentation: Generates the CAST to be used to appropriately range partition a table

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.gen_sql_text
documentation:
Generate a SQL statement appropriate for running in the backend.
column_names: A list of column names to select, these are dimensions when aggregating.
              The values have enclose_identifier() applied so you cannot pass expressions or literals.
filter_clauses: A list of 'col=something' strings.
measures: A list of column names to have agg_fns applied to.
agg_fns: A list of agg fns, e.g. ['sum', 'max']

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.get_distinct_column_values
documentation:
Run SQL to get distinct values for a list of columns.
The columns_to_cast_to_string parameter is because our HiveServer2 DBAPI does not support nanoseconds
therefore we bring certain data types back as strings to avoid truncating to milliseconds.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.get_max_column_values
documentation:
Get max value for a set of columns.
optimistic_prune_clause is used to limit the partitions scanned when looking for existing rows.
If no data is returned then we fall back to scanning without the clause optimistic_prune_clause
is only applicable for single column partition schemes.
The columns_to_cast_to_string parameter is because our HiveServer2 DBAPI does not support nanoseconds
therefore we bring certain data types back as strings to avoid truncating to milliseconds.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.get_partition_columns
documentation: Returns a list of column objects for partition columns

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.get_table_ddl
documentation:
Return CREATE TABLE DDL as a string (or a list of strings split on CR if as_list=True)
terminate_sql: Adds any executing character to the end of the SQL (e.g. a semi-colon)
for_replace: If the backend supports it this includes the equivalent of:
    CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.get_table_partitions
documentation:
The return of this is currently matching whatever HiveTable() does
In order to implement for any new backend we're going to need to dig
into this and decide on the most appropriate structure for the future

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.get_table_row_count
documentation:
Note that this is interrogating the table in order to get the actual row count
not getting the value from stats or some potentially stale location
filter_clause does NOT include "WHERE" because that could be backend specific

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.get_table_row_count_from_metadata
documentation:
Note that this is getting the row count from table metadata, such as stats
It may or may not be stale depending on the backend system

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.get_table_size
documentation:
Return the size of the table in bytes.
no_cache: Allows us to bypass any backend caching for systems that automatically do that.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.get_table_size_and_row_count
documentation:
Return a tuple of size in bytes and number of rows for a table.
Some backends can avoid a round trip if the developer knows both values are required.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.get_table_sort_columns
documentation: Get sort/cluster column names for a table, either as a list of names or a CSV.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.get_table_stats
documentation:
For historic reasons this is expected to return two objects:
    (table_stats, column_stats)
    table_stats: num_rows, num_bytes, avg_row_len
    column_stats: col_name, ndv, num_nulls, avg_col_len, low_value, high_value, max_col_len
See HiveTableStats class for more details

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.get_table_and_partition_stats
documentation:
This has been broken out from get_table_stats() because the output looks pretty Hadoop specific
If we decide we need this functionality for another backend then we should look at the format
and decide what to do

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.insert_literal_values
documentation:
Used to insert specific data into a table. The table should already exist.
literal_list: A list of rows to insert (a list of lists).
              The row level lists should contain the exact right number of columns.
column_list: The column specs for the columns to be inserted, if left blank this will default to all
             columns in the table
Disclaimer: This code is used in testing and generating the sequence table. It is not robust enough to
            be a part of any Offload Transport

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.is_nan_sql_expression
documentation: Return a SQL expression testing if an expression is NaN (not-a-number).

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.is_valid_partitioning_data_type
documentation:
Checks if a data type is valid for use as a backend partition column.
This is not saying what the backend supports, it is what GOE support, some
data types may end up being converted to something else via a GOE_PART column.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.is_valid_sort_data_type
documentation: Checks if a data type is valid for use as a backend sort/cluster column.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.is_valid_storage_compression
documentation: Checks if requested --storage-compression is valid for use by the backend.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.is_valid_storage_format
documentation: Checks if requested --storage-format is valid for use by the backend.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.length_sql_expression
documentation:
Return a SQL expression to get the length of column_expression, e.g.:
LENGTH(column_name)

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.list_databases
documentation:
Return a list of database names, optionally filtered.
db_name_filter: A string used to filter db names. May contain wildcard character * but no other wildcards.
case_sensitive: Defines case sensitivity of db_name_filter.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.list_tables
documentation:
Return a list of tables names within a specific db, optionally filtered by table name.
table_name_filter: A string used to filter names. May contain wildcard character * but no other wildcards.
case_sensitive: Defines case sensitivity of table_name_filter.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.list_udfs
documentation:
Return a list of UDFs within a specific db, optionally filtered by UDF name.
    [ [ udf_name, return_type ], ]
udf_name_filter: A string used to filter names. May contain wildcard character * but no other wildcards.
case_sensitive: Defines case sensitivity of table_name_filter.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.list_views
documentation:
Return a list of views names within a specific db, optionally filtered by name.
view_name_filter: A string used to filter names. May contain wildcard character * but no other wildcards.
case_sensitive: Defines case sensitivity of table_name_filter.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.max_decimal_integral_magnitude
documentation: The maximum number of digits permitted to the left of the decimal place for the DECIMAL based data type.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.max_decimal_precision
documentation: The maximum value of precision permitted for the relevant DECIMAL/NUMBER based data type.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.max_decimal_scale
documentation:
The maximum value of data_scale permitted for the relevant DECIMAL/NUMBER based data type.
Optional data type parameter for backends with multiple DECIMAL data types.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.max_datetime_value
documentation: Return the maximum datetime value that can be stored by this backend as datetime64().

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.max_datetime_scale
documentation: Return the maximum scale (number of decimal places for seconds) that can be stored by this backend.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.max_partition_columns
documentation: Return the maximum number of partition columns for the backend system.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.max_sort_columns
documentation: Return the maximum number of sort columns for the backend system

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.max_table_name_length
documentation: The maximum number of characters permitted in a table name

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.min_datetime_value
documentation: Return the minimum datetime value that can be stored by this backend as datetime64().

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.native_integer_types
documentation: Return a list of native integer data types for the backend.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.partition_column_requires_synthetic_column
documentation: Returns True if the backend column requires a synthetic column if it is used for partitioning.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.populate_sequence_table
documentation:
Populate options.sequence_table_name up to target_seq.
split_by_cr: This option is a bit of a fudge but allows a calling program to parse the output.
             The idea being that, on some backends, this method may produce lengthy output
             which we may not want on screen. With split_by_cr the calling program has the
             opportunity to split the executed text by cr and only log some lines.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.regexp_extract_sql_expression
documentation: Return a SQL expression returning a REGEXP_EXTRACT SQL expression.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.role_exists
documentation: Check the role exists, returns True/False

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.set_session_db
documentation:
Switch the default db for a session to db_name.
Code should always explicitly specify object prefixes therfore usage of this method ought to be rare.
Typical SQL for this would be "USE ..."

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.supported_date_based_partition_granularities
documentation:
Return a list of supported granularities for date based partitioning.
The list should be a subset of:
  [PART_COL_GRANULARITY_YEAR, PART_COL_GRANULARITY_MONTH, PART_COL_GRANULARITY_DAY]

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.supported_partition_function_parameter_data_types
documentation: Return a list of backend data types supported as partition function parameters

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.supported_partition_function_return_data_types
documentation: Return a list of backend data types supported as the result of a partition function

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.synthetic_partition_numbers_are_string
documentation: Are synthetic partition column values for numeric source columns stored as strings, True/False

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.table_distribution
documentation: Get the distribution setting for a table. This may not apply to some backends, it was added for Synapse

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.table_exists
documentation: Return bool depending whether the table exists or not.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.table_has_rows
documentation: Return bool depending whether the table has rows or not.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.target_version
documentation:
Return version of the backend SQL engine in x.y.z format that can be used by GOEVersion().
This is different to backend_version() even though it appears similar in function.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.to_backend_literal
documentation:
Translate a Python value to a backend literal.
Examples where this may act are dates (which may need specific formatting) and
string (need quoting with a specific character).
Other types are likely just a pass through.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.transform_encrypt_data_type
documentation: Return a backend data type matching the output of an encryption expression.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.transform_null_cast
documentation: Return a SQL expression to cast a NULL value in place of a column.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.transform_tokenize_data_type
documentation: Return a backend data type matching the output of a tokenization expression.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.udf_details
documentation:
Returns a list of UdfDetails for requested UDF name.
We return a list because some backends can overload UDFs.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.udf_installation_os
documentation:
Copies any libraries required to support UDFs from our software package to wherever they should be.
Returns a list of commands executed.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.udf_installation_sql
documentation:
Executes any SQL commands required for UDF installation.
Returns a list of commands executed, in dry_run mode that's all it does.
udf_db can be empty which means we won't specify one and pickup a default.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.udf_installation_test
documentation:
Executes any SQL commands to test each UDF.
udf_db can be empty which means we won't specify one and pickup a default.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.valid_canonical_override
documentation:
Present has a number of options for overriding the default canonical mapping in to_canonical_column().
This method validates the override.
column: the source backend column object.
canonical_override: either a canonical column object or a GOE_TYPE_... data type.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.to_canonical_column
documentation: Translate a backend column to an internal GOE column.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.from_canonical_column
documentation: Translate an internal GOE column to a backend column.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.is_capability_supported
documentation: capability config

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.canonical_date_supported
documentation:
Does the backend have a pure date data type (no time part).
Note that there is an Impala override for this.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.canonical_float_supported
documentation: Does the backend have a pure 4 byte binary floating point data type

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.canonical_time_supported
documentation:
Does the backend have a pure time data type (no date part).
Note that there is an Impala override for this.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.case_sensitive_identifiers
documentation: Does the backend have case sensitive db/table name identifiers

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.column_stats_set_supported
documentation: Note that there is an Impala override for this

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.default_sort_columns_to_primary_key
documentation: Does this backend suit clustering on primary key if there's no user specified alternative.

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.drop_column_supported
documentation: Note that there is an Impala override for this

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.filesystem_scheme_abfs_supported
documentation: Note that there is an Impala override for this

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.filesystem_scheme_adl_supported
documentation: Note that there is an Impala override for this

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.filesystem_scheme_s3a_supported
documentation: Note that there is an Impala override for this

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.ranger_supported
documentation: Note that there is an Impala version specific override for this

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.sentry_supported
documentation: Note that there is an Impala version specific override for this

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.sorted_table_supported
documentation: Note that there is an Impala override for this

methods.name: goe.offload.backend_api.BackendApiInterface.sql_microsecond_predicate_supported
documentation: Can the orchestration SQL engine cope with literal predicates with microsecond precision

Abstract base class which acts as an interface for backend specific sub-classes
no_caching: Prevents state from being cached between calls. When testing we don't want caching
do_not_connect: Prevents the object from automatically connecting to a backend, required for unit tests
BackendApiInterface

functions.name: goe.offload.operation.partition_controls.default_granularity_for_matching_rdbms_list_partition_key

functions.name: goe.offload.operation.partition_controls.derive_partition_digits
documentation: Find common partition digits used across a set of partition columns.

functions.name: goe.offload.operation.partition_controls.offloading_date_as_string
documentation: Return True if we are offloading an RDBMS date to a backend string

functions.name: goe.offload.operation.partition_controls.validate_offload_partition_columns
documentation: Default and validate requested backend partition columns

functions.name: goe.offload.operation.partition_controls.validate_offload_partition_functions
documentation:
Validate partition functions offload option has valid contents.
Cannot check functions are compatible with the partition source column(s) because this will be called too early,
but we can check it exists and has a valid structure.
Returns a normalised value for offload_partition_functions, e.g. it may comes in as a string and leave as a list

functions.name: goe.offload.operation.partition_controls.part_cols_have_matching_synthetic_expression
documentation:
Identify when two synthetic columns can be a direct comparison in join pushdown.
Doesn't use source_column_name because that could be different, we have to trust the user when they
chose the source columns as join columns.

GLOBAL FUNCTIONS

functions.name: goe.offload.operation.data_type_controls.char_semantics_override_map
documentation: Return a dictionary map of char semantics overrides

functions.name: goe.offload.operation.data_type_controls.date_min_max_incompatible
documentation:
Return True if it's possible for the source data to hold dates that are incompatible
with the target system
Currently only looking at the min when checking this. We could add max checks to this
in the future

functions.name: goe.offload.operation.data_type_controls.offload_source_to_canonical_mappings
documentation:
Take source/RDBMS columns and translate them into intermediate canonical columns.
This could be from:
    User specified options: canonical_overrides.
    Source schema attributes: offload_source_table.to_canonical_column().
    Data type sampling: offload_source_table.sample_rdbms_data_types().

functions.name: goe.offload.operation.data_type_controls.report_data_type_control_options_to_simulate_sampling
documentation:
Users requested the outcome of sampling should be logged to avoid resampling if an offload is re-run
An example of when this is useful is running an offload in preview mode and then running in execute mode,
this will run sampling twice and in some cases can be frustrating

classes.name: goe.offload.format_literal.FormatLiteralInterface

methods.name: goe.offload.format_literal.FormatLiteralInterface.format_literal
documentation:
Return a string containing a correctly formatted literal for data_type.
Without a data type the code should infer a literal from the Python type.

methods.name: goe.offload.format_literal.FormatLiteralInterface._strip_unused_time_scale
documentation:
If there are fractional seconds then remove farthest right zeros because
Oracle might pass through "2030-01-02 00:00:00.000003000" which a backend
may reject because of the trailing zeros.

Used to format literals correctly for the specific implementation.

modules.name: goe.offload.synthetic_partition_literal
documentation:
SyntheticPartitionLiteral class for generating Python string literals for synthetic partition columns

The methods will give the same outcome as BackendTableInterface implementations do via SQL except this class is
focused on Python variables, not backend SQL engines.

This initially started out as a collection of global functions but became a class because I wanted some methods to
be private and using underscore naming for "global" functions seemed wrong.

functions.name: goe.offload.synthetic_partition_literal.gen_number_integral_floor_literal
documentation:
Python literal matching the outcome of BackendTableInterface._gen_synthetic_part_number_floor_sql_expr()
for an integral numeric synthetic column.

classes.name: goe.offload.synthetic_partition_literal.SyntheticPartitionLiteral

methods.name: goe.offload.synthetic_partition_literal.SyntheticPartitionLiteral._gen_date_as_string_literal
documentation: Python literal matching the outcome of BackendTableInterface._gen_synthetic_part_date_as_string_sql_expr()

documentation:
Python literal matching the outcome of
BackendTableInterface._gen_synthetic_partition_date_truncated_sql_expr()

methods.name: goe.offload.synthetic_partition_literal.SyntheticPartitionLiteral._gen_synthetic_literal_function
documentation:
Returns a Python function to achieve the same outcome as BackendTableInterface implementations would in SQL.
For example a month granularity fn that converts:
    datetime64('2011-01-01 12:12:12.1234') -> '2011-01'

methods.name: goe.offload.synthetic_partition_literal.SyntheticPartitionLiteral.gen_synthetic_literal_function
documentation:
Generate a Python function which can be used to create a synthetic partition literal
partition_column can be a column name, which must exist in table_columns, or a column object.
PUBLIC METHODS
SyntheticPartitionLiteral

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi._close_cursor
documentation:
After a DDL/DML operation this is raising and logging:
    "Exception closing cursor: Attempt to use a closed cursor."
Those operations have None in the description property on the cursor so we
check for it before trying to close.

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi._create_statistics_sql
documentation: Return SQL to create statistics object on a column

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi._create_table_columns_clause
documentation:
Trivial helper for SQL text consistency.
Not using BackendApi common method due to Synapse requiring COLLATE support.

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi._create_table_sql_text
documentation:
Return SQL text to create a Synapse table.
See abstract method create_table() for more description.

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi._execute_ddl_or_dml
documentation:
Run SQL that has no result set, e.g. DDL, sql can be a str or a list of SQLs.
This function returns the SQL statements that were executed.
Exception will be raised upon failure.
Parameters:
    sync: None means do nothing. True/False mean act as appropriate.
    query_options: key/value pairs for session settings.
    profile: if True we will log a profile (if possible) *after the first* SQL statement.

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi._execute_global_session_parameters
documentation:
Execute global backend session parameters.
self._global_session_parameters is set in the super interface class and merges backend session parameters
passed in from the command line and any _fixed_session_parameters set in this class.

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi._execute_query_fetch_x
documentation:
Run a query and fetch results
query_options: key/value pairs for session settings
query_params: list of bind values for 1..n params in sql
log_level: None = no logging. Otherwise VERBOSE/VVERBOSE etc
time_sql: If logging then log SQL elapsed time
not_when_dry_running: Some SQL will only work in execute mode, such as queries on a load table
as_dict: When True each row is returned as a dict keyed by column name rather than a tuple/list.

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi._fixed_session_parameters
documentation: No fixed session parameters currently required for Synapse

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi._format_query_options
documentation:
Format SET commands for Synapse.
query_options: key/value pairs for session settings. E.g.

See docstring on BackendApi for more details.

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi.backend_report_info

Reporting information about the type of backend and how to display
its configuration details (e.g. Synapse host:port).

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi.compute_stats
documentation:
Synapse has statistics objects on each column that we must maintain.
If for_columns is True we will create the statistics objects on each column.
If for_columns is None/False we will update the statistics on the entire table.
We always use a fixed 5% sample for now. We may turn --sample-stats into an offload/present
option in the future to allow the sample size to be parameterised (GOE-2133).

properties: not applicable
comment: not applicable

See abstract method for more description

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi.create_view

Create a Synapse view
See create_view() description on interface for parameter descriptions.
SQL below does not use enclose_object_reference() due to:
    Parse error at line: 1, column: 13:
    VIEW does not allow a database name to be specified. (103021) (SQLExecDirectW)')

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi.get_session_option
documentation:
Not implemented on Synapse
SESSIONPROPERTY() looks like the right function to achieve this but on Synapse:
    'SESSION_PROPERTY' is not a recognized built-in function name.

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi.get_table_ddl
documentation:
Mock up Synapse table DDL based on how GOE code would create a table.
This is not ideal but Synapse doesn;t have a readily available DDL retrieval method. What this code
does is derive the inputs as they should have been at table creation time and feeds them back through
the same code. The flaw is, if we change our code generator we'll get a different output from this code.
This is not widely used (at the time of writing) so should be sufficient.

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi.get_table_partition_count
documentation: Table partitioning not implemented at this stage on Synapse

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi.get_table_partitions
documentation: Table partitioning not implemented at this stage on Synapse

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi.get_table_sort_columns
documentation:
Synapse can have
    a) CLUSTERED COLUMNSTORE INDEX ORDER (column [,...n])
    b) CLUSTERED INDEX ( { index_column_name [ ASC | DESC ] } [ ,...n ] ) -- default is ASC
This should cater for them both

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi.get_table_stats
documentation:
For historic reasons this is expected to return two objects:
    (table_stats, column_stats)
    table_stats: num_rows, num_bytes, avg_row_len
    column_stats: col_name, ndv, num_nulls, avg_col_len, low_value, high_value, max_col_len
See HiveTableStats class for more details

Note SS@2021-09-20: It's possible to get the num_nulls, low_value and high_value with a call
    to DBCC SHOW_STATISTICS (?, ?) WITH HISTOGRAM. However this has not been implemented due
    to the fact that the result set would be equal to the number of rows for a unique column.
    We only need the min and the max from this result set but there is no way to do this
    in the backend and we don't want to return potentially millions of rows per column.

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi.insert_literal_values
documentation:
Insert rows into Synapse table using INSERT ... VALUES. Only suitable for low volumes.
Cannot add any CASTs when using INSERT ... VALUES. Statements fail with:
    Msg 104334, Level 16, State 1, Line 1
    Insert values statement can contain only constant literal values or variable references.
Also, even though MSSQL supports multi-row INSERT ... VALUES statements, Synapse does not:
    https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/t-sql/statements/insert-transact-sql?view=sql-server-ver15#b-inserting-multiple-rows-of-data
    Note: The table value constructor is not supported in Azure Synapse Analytics.
For this reason we insert a UNION ALL select statement, like we do on Hive.

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi.is_nan_sql_expression
documentation: If we reach here something has gone wrong so raise an exception

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi.is_valid_partitioning_data_type
documentation: Table partitioning not implemented at this stage on Synapse

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi.list_databases
documentation:
Case-sensitivity of the LIKE function in Synapse is determined by the case sensitivity of
the collation of the database. So for a case-insensitive database there can only be one
database name and it will either be stored in lower or upper case, and LIKE will return it
regardless of the case of the literal passed in to the LIKE function. For a case-sensitive
database there can be two databases with the same name and different case. The LIKE function
will only match the case of the literal passed in.

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi.list_tables
documentation: case_sensitive is handled by the database collation. See list_databases comment for why

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi.list_views
documentation: case_sensitive is handled by the database collation. See list_databases comment for why

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi.max_datetime_scale
documentation:
Synapse SQL supports accuracy of 100ns
https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/t-sql/functions/date-and-time-data-types-and-functions-transact-sql?view=azure-sqldw-latest

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi.max_partition_columns

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi.max_sort_columns
documentation: Returning SORT_COLUMNS_UNLIMITED as clustered columnstore indexes include all columns

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi.min_datetime_value
documentation:
This is used to understand whether the minimum data we might offload can cause issues in Synapse.
We offload canonical DATE/TIMESTAMP to Synapse DATE/DATETIME2 so the value returned below is the minimum
for those types and not Synapse DATETIME which has minimum 1753-01-01. Offload will never use Synapse
DATETIME therefore this is fine.

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi._option_clause_valid_for_sql_text
documentation:
Return True if we support adding OPTION clause to sql_text.
Ideally this is covered by the developer not asking for a profile when it doesn't make sense.
But, in some cases, we may pass a list of SQLs where some are compatible and some are not.

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi.refresh_table_files
documentation: No requirement to re-scan files for a table on Synapse but drop cache because that will be stale

methods.name: goe.offload.microsoft.synapse_backend_api.BackendSynapseApi.decimal_scale_validation_expression
documentation:
Return a SQL expression to validate the scale length of string based staging columns holding decimal data.
The expression ignores data in scientific notation because the scale in those literals bears no relation
to the actual scale of the value.
Uses instr/split type functions because Synapse does not support regular expressions!

Depending on value in connection_options.db_type the construct method will return
the appropriate class for the DB in use.
We import frontend classes inside the factory function to avoid loading libraries we'll never use.

the engine used to write to the Avro file but the engine used to validate it and insert into the final table.

methods.name: goe.offload.bigquery.bigquery_backend_table.BackendBigQueryTable._create_load_table
documentation:
Create the staging/load table in BigQuery
Defining a URI for location is tricky. Initially we had *.avro but Avro files written by Spark ThriftServer
didn't match. Restrictions on the URI:
    For Google Cloud Storage URIs: Each URI can contain one '*' wildcard character and
    it must come after the 'bucket' name.

Examples of Avro files we have to deal with:
Query Import:
    Staged path: gs://bucket/.../part-m-00000.avro
spark-submit:
    Staged path: gs://bucket/.../_SUCCESS
    Staged path: gs://bucket/.../part-00000-009774b7-f994-45c9-870f-a346aaa5e59c-c000.avro
Spark ThriftServer:
    Staged path: gs://bucket/.../part-00000-1267a519-b931-445a-beed-2ee32f4c4c00-c000

We can't control file name for Spark ThriftServer:
    The files that are written by the Hive job are valid Avro files, however, MapReduce doesn't add the
    standard .avro extension. If you copy these files out, you'll likely want to rename them with .avro.

We've gone with part* to identify Avro files.
PRIVATE METHODS

classes.name: goe.offload.offload_source_data.OffloadSourcePartition
documentation:
Holds a list of partitions for an RDBMS table:
Basis is this is format Oracle uses, this should be refined in future to be more generic
[
  partition name
, high value CSV string (for Oracle dba_tab_partitions.high_value)
, high values as Python values
, partition size in bytes
, number of rows in partition (according to stats)
, HVs as individual strings
, is the boundary a common boundary (True or False and only applies when subpartition range offloads are in play)
, [ is a list of subpartitions for the partition (only applies for partition range offloads) ]
]
OffloadSourcePartitions

methods.name: goe.offload.offload_source_data.OffloadSourceDataInterface._make_rpa_hwm_gte_clause
documentation:
If this is a partition append incremental offload then we can attempt to build a where clause to optimise
the backend scan to get the current data HWM.
Only returns a clause if:
  - RDBMS table has a single partition column (i.e. not multi-column-partitioning, nothing to do
    with sub-partitioning). This is because the hwm clause for multi-col would be excessively complex.
  - There is previous metadata.
  - We're not resetting.
  - The relevant backend partition column is a synthetic one, otherwise we trust backend engine to optimise.

methods.name: goe.offload.offload_source_data.OffloadSourceDataInterface._select_target_table_max
documentation:
Get highest point of data stored in target table and store in state
Impyla/cx_Oracle both truncate nanoseconds hence TIMESTAMP columns are returned as string
optimistic_prune_clause is used to limit the partitions scanned when looking for existing rows
    If no data is returned then we resort to scanning without the clause
    optimistic_prune_clause is only applicable for single column partition schemes
Also returns the max value for convenience

and type.

methods.name: goe.offload.offload_source_data.OffloadSourceDataIpaRange._normalise_rpa_partition_name
documentation:
Convert a partition name to a HV and then convert that HV to a tuple used for partition filtering
Locates the partition in _source_partitions so this must be populated
Doesn't validate the number of partition columns or data types because HV is coming directly from RDBMS data dictionary

methods.name: goe.offload.offload_source_data.OffloadSourceDataIpaRange._rpa_partition_already_offloaded
documentation:
This method is used to differentiate between true RANGE and LIST_AS_RANGE.
rdbms_min <= backend_max: If there's data in Oracle older than backend max then current partition must
                          be offloaded and we'll stop iterating when we find a HWM beyond backend max
p.partition_values_python <= backend_max: If the HV for current partition is older than backend max then it must
                                          be offloaded this latter check is required for truncated/empty partitions
partition_already_marked_offloaded: When looping through partitions the next partition beyond the one with the HV
                                    matching backend data is also offloaded, due to less-than nature of RANGE HV.

methods.name: goe.offload.offload_source_data.OffloadSourceDataIpaRange._rpa_partitions_already_offloaded
documentation:
Return partitions already offloaded based on a HWM and min/max values
i.e. this is not comparing all partitions but looking for the HWM in the backend and assuming
     all older RDBMS partitions have been offloaded

methods.name: goe.offload.offload_source_data.OffloadSourceDataIpaRange._set_incremental_high_values
documentation:
Identify a HWM partition for a RANGE 90/10 config.
HWM is filtered HWM or highest partition to be offloaded or what's already been offloaded.
Filtered HWM is checked against source partitions because the HWM may be positioned anywhere (100/10).

documentation: When RANGE offloading at subpartition level we need to ensure that any HWM for offload is a valid common boundary

methods.name: goe.offload.offload_source_data.OffloadSourceDataIpaList._set_incremental_high_values
documentation:
Identify incremental high values for this offload for a 90/10 config
Incremental high values means:
  - partitions matching the user requested filters
  - plus what hybrid metadata thinks has been offloaded (in case RDBMS partitions have been dropped)
Does not add:
  - partitions in process of being offloaded. For FULL it is possible to offload partitions the user
    didn't specify. That's fine but we don't want them in the metadata
Uses str() around each literal in order to be sure no mix of str/unicode
De-dupes the final list before sorting

The function first checks if the user is offloading a DEFAULT partition before
going on to check for other partitions they may have requested. We do it that way
around to avoid duck typing issues when comparing offload_constants.PART_OUT_OF_LIST with other types

documentation:
This method is used to differentiate between true RANGE and LIST_AS_RANGE.
We can ignore partition_already_marked_offloaded because LIST_AS_RANGE HVs are like-for-like.

This is a limited extension of OffloadSourceDataIpaRange used when customers have partitioned Oracle
by LIST but to mimic RANGE partitioning.
This class inherits most functionality from Range but with these additions:
    1) RDBMS partitions are sorted by HIGH_VALUE in order to ensure matched RANGE behaviour
    2) HIGH_VALUE logic is changed from <= to < to match RANGE logic
OffloadSourceDataIpaListAsRange

modules.name: goe.offload.bigquery.bigquery_backend_api
documentation:
BigQueryBackendApi: Library for logic/interaction with a remote BigQuery backend.
This module enforces an interface with common, highlevel, methods and an implementation
for each supported remote system, e.g. Impala, Hive, Google BigQuery.

methods.name: goe.offload.bigquery.bigquery_backend_api.BackendBigQueryApi._check_kms_key_name
documentation:
Use startswith() to verify custom key for key name.
Example of custom key name:
    projects/goe-teamcity/locations/us-west3/keyRings/krname/cryptoKeys/etl5
Example of what we get from a query_job object:
    projects/goe-teamcity/locations/us-west3/keyRings/krname/cryptoKeys/etl5/cryptoKeyVersions/1
It's worth noting that this has not always been the case, BigQuery behaviour has changed in the past.

methods.name: goe.offload.bigquery.bigquery_backend_api.BackendBigQueryApi._get_query_profile
documentation:
On BigQuery query_identifier is a QueryJob object.
Slot Usage: This is a statistic derived by us. Text from GOE-1797:
    When looking at QueryStatistics, slot usage is defined as:
        ceil(slotMillis/(endTime-creationTime))
    Using the attributes available from this API, the calculation would be:
        ceil(slot_millis-(ended-created))
    Where ended and created are DATETIME, meaning that the interval will need to be converted to ms.

table is a table object as returned by self._get_bq_table().

documentation:
Return a ColumnPartitionInfo object (or None) based on native partition settings.
BigQuery only allows a single partition column so we could, in theory, ignore
both column and position parameters. We don't do this though and use column as a
sanity check and position because then we're behaving like other backends.

methods.name: goe.offload.bigquery.bigquery_backend_api.BackendBigQueryApi.gen_default_numeric_column
documentation:
Return a default best-fit numeric column for generic usage.
Used when we can't really know or influence a type, e.g. AVG() output in AAPD.
data_scale=0 can be used if the outcome is known to be integral.
On BigQuery data_scale=0 means use BIGQUERY_TYPE_INT64, otherwise data_scale influences whether
we use NUMERIC or BIGNUMERIC.

documentation:
Gets a list of column objects for partition column.
In BigQuery there can only be a single column in the list.
As of bigquery client version 1.24.0 integer range partitioning
is not visible, therefore we're falling back on the raw JSON until
the partition scheme moves past beta and presumably becomes visible.

methods.name: goe.offload.bigquery.bigquery_backend_api.BackendBigQueryApi.get_table_partitions

methods.name: goe.offload.bigquery.bigquery_backend_api.BackendBigQueryApi.insert_literal_values
documentation:
Insert an array of literals into a table using INSERT...VALUES(...),(...),(...).
We've done this for simplicity due to two issues:
1) BigQuery issue: https://github.com/googleapis/google-cloud-python/issues/5539
    "The issue is eventual consistency with the backend. Replacing the table, while it has the
     same table_id, represents a new table in terms of it's internal UUID and thus backends may
     deliver to the "old" table for a short period (typically a few minutes)."
   This means we can have problems with this method of inserting rows, it is likely only suitable for testing.
2) Python does not support encoding Decimal to JSON until Python 3.10:
    https://bugs.python.org/issue16535
   Which gives us issue with this API:
    site-packages/google/cloud/bigquery/client.py", line 3421, in insert_rows
        return self.insert_rows_json(table, json_rows, **kwargs)
        ...
    json/encoder.py", line 179, in default
        raise TypeError(f'Object of type {o.__class__.__name__} '
    TypeError: Object of type Decimal is not JSON serializable
